metadata.title: Why one generic CLI can drive an API it has never seen
metadata.purpose: mcpcurl introspects the server's tool schema and feeds it to a command builder that emits typed flags, validation, help text, and execution — the schema IS the interface definition.
metadata.clock: narration (Kokoro af_kore) — master clock; conform each beat to measured audio
metadata.prerequisites: JSON schema basics, MCP, CLI argument parsing
metadata.visual_object: A JSON schema document morphing into a CLI help screen with `--owner`, `--repo`, `--issue_number` appearing as typed options.
metadata.hook: A single command-line tool runs against any MCP server, with correct typed flags for tools it was never coded against — how does it adapt at runtime?

beats.narration_text: `mcpcurl tools get_issue --owner golang --repo go` returns real results, though mcpcurl has no built-in knowledge of `get_issue` or its flags.
beats.scaffold_note: from Key case — cold open on the concrete instance

beats.narration_text: Having a tool's schema should let you generate a fully typed CLI with validation — this tool does exactly that; why is the schema alone enough to synthesize the interface?
A single command-line tool runs against any MCP server, with correct typed flags for tools it was never coded against — how does it adapt at runtime?
mcpcurl introspects the server's tool schema and feeds it to a command builder that emits typed flags, validation, help text, and execution — the schema IS the interface definition.
beats.visual: A JSON schema document morphing into a CLI help screen with `--owner`, `--repo`, `--issue_number` appearing as typed options.
beats.scaffold_note: the resolving mechanism; orbits the visual object

beats.narration_text: A "weather" server exposes `get_temp {city:string, units:enum[C,F]}`; mcpcurl auto-builds `weather tools get_temp --city Seattle --units C` and rejects `--units X`.
mcpcurl introspects the server's tool schema and feeds it to a command builder that emits typed flags, validation, help text, and execution — the schema IS the interface definition.
